page: Hetzner Cloud cpx62 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running on a QEMU hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation and a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 31.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled 640 GB local SSD. No dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this tier.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1968.0 and a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 3035.28. Memory performance is also a key strength, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 277.56 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 137.51 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 is highly efficient, scoring 198.15 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall into the average tier, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cpx62 offers a high density of compute cores and local SSD storage relative to its resource tier. The shared CPU model introduces potential performance variability under sustained multi-core loads, representing a tradeoff for workloads requiring guaranteed, continuous CPU performance. However, the strong memory bandwidth and single-core capabilities make it highly competitive for memory-bound tasks.

This server is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, medium-scale text and image processing, and lightweight LLM inference tasks using small to medium models. It also functions effectively for general-purpose application hosting and development environments where dedicated CPU resources are not strictly required.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx62 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 640 GB of local SSD storage. Running on a QEMU hypervisor, this server delivers strong single-core CPU performance and excellent large-block memory bandwidth, though its shared CPU allocation places multi-core performance in the average tier. The instance lacks dedicated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. It represents a cost-efficient option for resource-dense workloads, trading dedicated CPU guarantees for high core and storage capacity. The cpx62 is best suited for static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight LLM inference using small to medium models.

31.0 GB of system memory and 640 GB of local SSD storage
